questions.question: What is reflection of light?
questions.correct_answer: The bouncing back of light when it strikes a surface
questions.explanation: When light hits a mirror or shiny surface, it returns in another direction. This is called reflection.

questions.question: What is a plane mirror?
questions.correct_answer: A flat reflecting surface that forms an image
questions.explanation: Plane mirrors form images that are upright, virtual, and the same size as the object.

questions.question: What is the law of reflection?
questions.correct_answer: The angle of incidence is equal to the angle of reflection
questions.explanation: The incoming light ray and the reflected ray make equal angles with the normal.

questions.question: What is lateral inversion?
questions.correct_answer: The left and right sides of an image are reversed in a mirror
questions.explanation: In a mirror image, the right side appears left and the left side appears right.

questions.question: What is a concave mirror?
questions.correct_answer: A mirror whose reflecting surface curves inward
questions.explanation: Concave mirrors can form both real and virtual images depending on the position of the object.

questions.question: What is a convex mirror?
questions.correct_answer: A mirror whose reflecting surface bulges outward
questions.explanation: Convex mirrors always form virtual, upright, and smaller images.

questions.question: Why are convex mirrors used as rear-view mirrors?
questions.correct_answer: Because they provide a wider field of view
questions.explanation: Convex mirrors allow drivers to see more area behind the vehicle.

questions.question: What is a lens?
questions.correct_answer: A transparent curved surface that bends light
questions.explanation: Lenses are used in optical instruments to form images.

questions.question: What is a convex lens?
questions.correct_answer: A lens that is thicker in the middle and converges light rays
questions.explanation: Convex lenses are used in magnifying glasses and cameras.

questions.question: What is a concave lens?
questions.correct_answer: A lens that is thinner in the middle and diverges light rays
questions.explanation: Concave lenses are used in spectacles to correct short-sightedness.

notes.title: Reflection of Light
notes.content: Reflection occurs when light rays bounce back from a surface such as a mirror.

notes.title: Types of Mirrors
notes.content: Mirrors can be plane mirrors, concave mirrors, or convex mirrors depending on the shape of the reflecting surface.

notes.title: Laws of Reflection
notes.content: The angle of incidence equals the angle of reflection and the incident ray, reflected ray, and normal lie in the same plane.

notes.title: Types of Lenses
notes.content: Lenses are mainly of two types: convex lenses and concave lenses.

notes.title: Image Formation
notes.content: Mirrors and lenses form images by reflecting or refracting light rays.

facts.fact: Plane mirrors form images that are the same size as the object.
Concave mirrors can focus light rays to a point called the focus.
Convex mirrors always produce smaller images.
Convex lenses are used in magnifying glasses and cameras.
